summary: Finalized the review-ready five-file public example package for safe web-to-Markdown capture. Re-ran the verification helper, confirmed required files, source coverage, provider neutrality, safety workflow coverage, and no detected private identifiers or local-only terms.
next_angle: Lifecycle completion; no further research is needed for this package unless a reviewer asks for a synthetic capture fixture.

sources.title: RFC 9309: Robots Exclusion Protocol
sources.note: Primary reference for robots.txt as crawler coordination and politeness guidance.

sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
sources.note: Primary reference for safe HTTP methods, redirect behavior, and user-agent semantics.

sources.title: Python urllib.parse documentation
sources.note: Official reference supporting defensive URL validation rather than relying on parser success.

sources.title: OWASP Server-Side Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Defensive reference for URL-controlled fetches, allowlists, parser issues, and network controls.

sources.title: OWASP LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Defensive reference for treating external content as untrusted model input.

sources.title: CommonMark Spec 0.31.2
sources.note: Primary reference for Markdown syntax and raw HTML constructs relevant to render-safety boundaries.

sources.title: Microsoft MarkItDown repository
sources.note: Illustrative converter reference only; the package remains converter-neutral.

findings.text: The candidate package contains the requested README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md files.
The package is written in English and is shaped for a public repository evaluator rather than as an internal workspace dump.
Automated verification passed for required files, public-safety scan, provider-neutral wording, seven source references, risk checklist coverage, workflow stages, and artifact checklist coverage.
The live capture transcript omission is an intentional publication-safety choice, documented as a limitation in the package rather than a blocker.

analysis_artifacts.note: Review-ready five-file candidate package intended for examples/web-capture-evaluation/.
Verification helper re-run during finalization to check required files, public-safety markers, coverage terms, and source coverage.
Machine-readable verification report showing all checks passed and no privacy issues were detected.

adequacy.goal_alignment: The finalized candidate directly answers the goal by packaging a provider-neutral public example that explains risks, workflow gates, and review artifacts for safe web-to-Markdown capture in agent research workflows.
adequacy.coverage_summary: Covered the requested risk model, gated workflow, artifact checklist, source references, public-safety constraints, and final package shape. No blocking gaps remain.

covered_requirements.requirement: Prepare a public-safe repository example about safe web-to-Markdown capture.
covered_requirements.evidence: The package contains README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md written for public repository evaluators.
The report and prompt cover URL/network containment, request limits, robots/politeness, conversion safety, Markdown handling, and prompt-injection boundaries.

covered_requirements.requirement: Explain the workflow an agent should follow.
covered_requirements.evidence: The research plan and final report use intake, preflight, fetch, convert, analyze, synthesize, and verify stage gates.

covered_requirements.requirement: Explain what artifacts make the example useful for users evaluating the project.
covered_requirements.evidence: The package lists source logs, fetch metadata, converted Markdown, analysis notes, final report, and verification notes.

covered_requirements.requirement: Use English and primary/official sources where possible.
covered_requirements.evidence: All candidate files are in English and cite RFCs, Python docs, OWASP guidance, CommonMark, and one illustrative converter reference.

covered_requirements.requirement: Avoid private paths, chat identifiers, tokens, personal memories, and local-only tool names.
covered_requirements.evidence: The verification report found no privacy issues in the package.

evidence_risks.risk: The package intentionally does not include a live capture transcript; reviewers who need a full fixture may later add synthetic or explicitly licensed sample content.

adequacy.recommended_next_angle: Complete the research lifecycle with the current package as the final candidate.

validation_evidence.reason: The package matches the requested public example topic and output shape.
No private identifiers or local-only names were detected by the verification helper.
The candidate contains exactly the requested five public example files.
The source set uses direct primary and official references for HTTP, robots, URL validation, SSRF, prompt injection, and Markdown behavior.
The only remaining limitation, no live capture transcript, is documented and does not block the requested workflow example.

final_report_markdown:
## Review Candidate

The final candidate is a five-file repository example for `examples/web-capture-evaluation/`:

- `README.md`
- `prompt.md`
- `research-plan.md`
- `sources.md`
- `final-report.md`

It is written in English, provider-neutral, source-backed, and designed for public repository review. Verification passed for required files, safety workflow coverage, source references, and absence of detected private identifiers or local-only tool names.

The package intentionally omits a live capture transcript to avoid redistributing unknown page content; `final-report.md` documents that as a limitation and suggests a future synthetic or explicitly licensed fixture.

finalization.inferred_user_need: A public-safe, review-ready example package that demonstrates safe web-to-Markdown capture for Research Mode users and repository evaluators.
nonblocking_defects: No live capture transcript is included; this is intentional for publication safety and documented as a limitation.
revisions: Re-ran the package verification helper during finalization; no file edits were required after the check passed.

validation_evidence.reason: All five requested package files are present.
The verification report lists no privacy issues.
The verification report passed all risk, workflow, artifact, and provider-neutrality checks.
All seven recorded source references are present in the package.
Manual inspection of the package files found a coherent public-facing example rather than raw internal research state.

last_validation_findings: The documented live-capture omission is a nonblocking publication-safety limitation.
